description: Screenshot annotations on a native app surface. Element anchoring works here the same way it does in a browser: app rects come from getElementRect, and the geometry provider scales them by capturedImageWidth/windowWidth — so no devicePixelRatio query is needed and display scaling falls out for free. On Windows the session is rooted at the app window, so getElementRect is already window-relative and no origin is subtracted (rebasing here would shift every annotation by the window's desktop position — proven against Character Map, where the error was exactly the window origin). macOS differs and DOES rebase onto the window origin; see appWindowOrigin in src/core/annotations/geometry.ts and app-annotations-macos.spec.json. Character Map is the target for the same reason as app-surfaces.spec.json: it's a real Win32 binary present on every Windows edition including Server CI runners. Gated to Windows hosts with the binary present; elsewhere the app preflight lands the context as SKIPPED, which is asserted behavior rather than a gap. The criteria restriction (selector/elementClass have no native equivalent) is pinned in test/annotations-geometry.test.js, since a spec can't assert FAIL.

tests.description: Annotate a native element by its display text, and a fixed position in the window.

steps.description: Outline and badge a native element found by display text.
Position-anchored annotations need no element lookup, so they work on every surface.
